content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 · SACRAMENTO, 95804 ··· 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

August 12, 1976

Ms. Barbara H. Suggert
Lillick McHose & Charles
Two Embarcadero Center
San Francisco, CA 94111

Dear Ms. Suggert:

     This letter responds to your inquiry of August 2, 1976,
concerning a Sierra Club lobbyist who serves as a member of several
citizens advisory groups.

     On the basis of the information contained in that letter,
the lobbyist is not an "agency official," Government Code section
82004, by virtue of service on those boards. Reports and pay-
ments for the time spent on the advisory board are not payments
as influence legislative or administrative action. However, the
Sierra Club should report the position or the lobbyist's entry
attributable to lobbying activities on Part 1 of the form 630,
"Payments Made to Lobbyists Employed or Retained." If the lobbyist
continues service on the advisory board in addition to serving
on the advisory boards, it would seem that no portion of
the salary is attributable to service on the advisory boards, and
that the lobbyist's entire salary should be reported on Part 1.

     I hope this information will be of assistance. I am
enclosing a copy of the Opinion requested by John U. Morrison, 2
State Bar of [illegible] (No. 75-096, dated August 9, 1976), in case you do not have
a copy of that opinion as another. Please contact me if you have
any questions.

     Sincerely,

[signature]
Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

Encl: Attorney Opinion No. 75-099

LILLICK McHOSE & CHARLES

ATTORNEYS AT LAW

CARLS [RAILLICK
WU TELEX 24-0806
TELECOPIER (415) 421-4627

TWO EMBARCADERO CENTER
SAN FRANCISCO 94111
(415) 421-4600

611 WEST SIXTH STREET
LOS ANGELES 90017
(213) 620-0000

1225 19TH STREET, N.W.
WASHINGTON, D.C. 20036
(202) 785-3288

SAN FRANCISCO

August 2, 1976


Ms. Natalie West
Fair Political Practices
Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Ms. West:

    By this letter we request written advice in accord-
ance with 2 Cal. Adm. Code §18329 with respect to the follow-
ing matter.

    A registered lobbyist in the employ of the Sierra
Club also serves as a member of several citizens advisory
committees. Specifically, the lobbyist sits on the following
advisory groups:

    1.  Water Advisory Panel to the Director of the
        Department of Water Resources;
    2.  Residential Building Standards Advisory
        Committee to the Energy Commission;
    3.  Environmental Task Force to the Attorney
        General;
    4.  Environmental Advisory Committee to the
        Southern California Air Pollution Control
        District;
    5.  Advisory Committee to the Water Resources
        Center of the University of California.

    With respect to the Water Advisory Panel, the
lobbyist is paid expenses; and on the Residential Building
Standards Advisory Committee all the members are paid a con-
sulting fee. No remuneration is received for services on the
latter three committees.

Ms. Natalie West
August 2, 1976
Page Two


     In each case the citizens committee involved makes
recommendations, which are then acted upon by the particular
agency or board. It is my understanding, and the understand-
ing of the Club lobbyist, that these committees are not
decision making bodies and their recommendations are not auto-
matically approved.

     The lobbyist attends meetings of these various advis-
ory committees during normal working hours, but it is expected
that all Sierra Club responsibilities be fulfilled. Appoint-
ment to the committees resulted from the lobbyist's continued
involvement and concern with environmental matters on the
national, state and local levels.

     In light of the Commission's recent Opinion as
requested by John C. Morrisey, PG&E Company (Opinion No. 75-
099), by virtue of service on these advisory committees is
this lobbyist an agency official within the meaning of
Government Code Section 82004? Must any portion of the lobby-
ist's salary be reported by the Sierra Club on its lobbyist
employer report as a payment to influence legislative or
administrative action for that time while the lobbyist is
serving on the advisory committees?

     Thank you for your consideration of this matter.
Please do not hesitate to contact the undersigned if you have
any questions.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        LILLICK McHOSE & CHARLES

                                        [signature]
                                        Barbara H. Buggert

57:2:SIE1 GEN

Buggert Lillick McChose & Charles Two Embarcadero Center San Francisco, CA 94111 Dear Ms. Buggert: This letter responds to your inquiry of August 2, 1976, concerning a Sierra Club lobbyist who serves as a member of several citizens advisory groups. On the basis of the information contained in that letter, the lobbyist is not an "agency official," Government Code Section 82004, by virtue of service on those boards. Moreover, salary payments for the time spent on the advisory board are not payments to influence legislative or administrative action. However, the Sierra Club should report the portion of the lobbyist's salary attributable to lobbying activities on Part I of the Form 650, "Payments Made to Lobbyists Employed or Retained." If the lobbyist fulfills all Sierra Club responsibilities in addition to serving on the advisory boards, it would seem that no portion of the salary is attributable to service on the advisory boards, and that the lobbyist's entire salary should be reported on Part I. I hope this information will be of assistance. I am enclosing a copy of the Opinion requested by John C. Morrissey, 2 FPPC Ops. 84 (No. 76-099, July 6, 1976), in case you do not have a copy of that opinion as adopted. Please contact me if you have any questions. Sincerely, Natalie E.
